demographics.living_situation: lives independently in own home, alone this evening

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My doctor's clinic sent me an automated text tonight saying my liver bloods came back abnormal, and now my right side is aching. Is this something serious? Do I need to go to hospital?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Got a text this evening about abnormal liver blood results and since then has noticed an ache on the right side under the ribs and feels a bit queasy; wants to know whether it is dangerous.

symptom_narrative.onset: started a few hours ago, right after I got the text message about my liver results
symptom_narrative.character: a dull, vague ache, not sharp
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: mild — maybe a 3 out of 10, it comes and goes
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: up under my ribs on the right side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: on and off, worse when I keep pressing on it to check
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting quietly and not thinking about it seems to ease it a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: pressing on the area and worrying about the blood test result
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a bit of nausea; no vomiting
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no, I have never had pain like this here before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can still do everything, but I am anxious and keep checking the spot

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I think my liver has been mentioned before at check-ups but I am not totally sure what it was about
past_medical_history.how_managed: my regular GP keeps an eye on it with blood tests

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: rarely drinks, none in the last week
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home alone this evening, feeling worried after the automated result message

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened that the abnormal blood test and the new pain mean something is seriously wrong with her liver and that she may need to go to hospital tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: asks repeated reassurance-seeking questions, focuses on the worst-case interpretation of the result

objective_data_offered.value: I don't feel hot and I haven't had the shivers or shakes
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective; no home thermometer used, so fever is not reliably excluded
